instruction:
Solve the integral:
$$
\int \frac{ \sin(x)^5 }{ \cos(x)^4 } \, dx
$$

Options:
A. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^2} + \cos(x)
B. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^4} + \cos(x)
C. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^2} + \sin(x)
D. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \sin(x)^2
E. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \sin(x)
F. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \sin(x)^3
G. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \sin(x)^6
H. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \sin(x)^4
I. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \cos(x)
J. C + \frac{2}{\cos(x)} + \frac{1}{3 \cdot (\cos(x))^3} + \sin(x)^5
